title: Isar
objectives: Explain Isar's NoSQL model + native performance
Query with indexes and full-text search

sections.markdown: Isar is a fast, cross-platform NoSQL database for Flutter/Dart — schemas defined by Dart classes with codegen, native binaries under the hood, and rich query + full-text + relation support.

Isar's edge: **write speed and cross-platform native perf** without dealing with SQL migrations. Great for mobile-first apps with lots of local data. Downside: smaller community/tooling than SQLite-backed Drift.
Doing multiple writes without a `writeTxn` — each write is its own transaction (slower). Batch writes inside one transaction.
Index only the fields you filter/sort on. Isar's queries use only one index at a time — compose your indexes to match your top queries.

flashcards.back: NoSQL documents defined by Dart classes with codegen; native binaries provide speed.
watch() on a collection or query returns a Stream that emits on changes.

options: A native cross-platform NoSQL DB for Dart

quiz.explain: Design indexes to match your top queries.

challenges.text: Define a collection and insert/read a document.
Add an index and query with filter().equalTo().
Model a one-to-many relation with links.
Add full-text search over a collection and watch results as a Stream.

interviewQuestions: How does Isar achieve high write throughput?
How do you index a query?
How do you handle schema changes?
